title: Fast-Food Nutrition Dataset
html:
The accumulated corpus of restaurant nutrition data — calories, macros, sodium, allergens, serving sizes — Yahir is assembling to back BroMenu / Brotein / anabolic scoring. The dataset's logic is top-down by anabolic merit, not bottom-up by restaurant catalog: the July 9 schema seed in Supabase was already loaded with 45+ items skewed toward breakfast/protein-heavy picks (Egg Bites, Protein Boxes, Egg White Wraps, Spicy McCrispy, Carved Turkey, Tuna). The data isn't "everything Chick-fil-A sells" — it's "things a anabolic scoring app would surface as good picks."

The bootstrap sprint runs July 17–18 2025 (restaurant menu scraping):

  • 2025 07 17 screenshot 2025 07 17 at 9 13 52 pm bb1a5662 — Chipotle (proof case: user-assembled, optimal-stack rankings computable from one page)
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 2 41 11 am f76d9c99 — Little Caesars (the trap: Stuffed Crazy Bread = 3,330 mg sodium)
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 3 10 37 am fedfeec5 — Wingstop (gold standard: 10g protein per ~95 cal, all 13 flavors)
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 13 am e3d1a9e8 / 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 23 am 02657aa9 — Taco Bell (the variant problem)

Earlier seeded chains visible in the schema view: Chick fil A, McDonald's, Starbucks, Subway. The restaurant-ID UUID convention (...000001, ...000002, ...000003, ...000025) suggests more chains exist that just don't have items in that particular view yet.

Two open questions live with this dataset: whether the schema needs separate menu_item_nutrition join tables (the visible columns are name + description only), and how variants get represented (boneless vs bone-in wings, sugar vs zero-sugar drinks, bowl vs burrito). Both push toward fuzzy-match canonicalization at insert time.

Source summaries

  • 2025 07 09 screenshot 2025 07 09 at 4 51 29 pm 720cb8bf — schema + seed items
  • 2025 07 17 screenshot 2025 07 17 at 9 13 52 pm bb1a5662 — Chipotle
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 2 41 11 am f76d9c99 — Little Caesars
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 3 10 37 am fedfeec5 — Wingstop
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 13 am e3d1a9e8 — Taco Bell pt 1
  • 2025 07 18 screenshot 2025 07 18 at 4 56 23 am 02657aa9 — Taco Bell pt 2

Connections

  • anabolic scoring — what consumes this dataset
  • restaurant menu scraping — how it's built
  • supabase — where it lives
  • bromenu / brotein — products that read from it
  • bromenu data model — proposed schema home
  • openai api — OCR + scoring layer
